name: Refugio Valdemanzano and Barranco de la Morca - circular from the Electric Central
name_original: Refugio Valdemanzano y Barranco de la Morca — circular desde la Central Eléctrica
region_intro: Hiking around Litago offers access to the diverse landscapes of Moncayo Natural Park in Spain. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including dense beech forests, rolling hills, and deep ravines. Hikers can explore paths leading to prominent summits and through natural cirques. This area provides a range of trails suitable for different experience levels, set within a protected natural environment. Best hiking trails around Litago The most popular hiking route is Hayedo Peña Roya — circular through the Moncayo Natural Park, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete, winding through a notable beech forest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabezo de la Mata from Fuente de los Frailes — circular through the Moncayo Natural Park, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path offering views from the Cabezo de la Mata hill. Local hikers also love the Refugio Valdemanzano and Barranco de la Morca - circular from the Electric Central, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through a ravine and past a mountain shelter, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Litago is defined by beech forests, hills, ravines, and mountain summits within Moncayo Natural Park. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest loops to more challenging summit ascents. The routes in Litago are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore Litago's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Litago?
A: Litago, situated within Moncayo Natural Park, offers a diverse range of hiking trails. You'll find paths winding through dense beech forests, over rolling hills, and into deep ravines. The area also features routes leading to prominent summits and through natural cirques, providing options for various experience levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Litago?
A: Yes, Litago offers several easier trails. Out of the 68 available routes, 9 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These trails allow you to experience the park's beauty without significant challenge.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Moncayo Natural Park near Litago?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974380061/hayedo-pena-roya-circular-through-the-moncayo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hayedo Peña Roya — circular through the Moncayo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop through a notable beech forest, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981868281/cabezo-de-la-mata-from-fuente-de-los-frailes-circular-through-the-moncayo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata from Fuente de los Frailes — circular through the Moncayo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views from the hill.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking near Litago?
A: Moncayo Natural Park is rich in natural beauty. You can explore fascinating geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6500103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Queiles River&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural highlight. For those interested in mountain shelters, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemanzano Nature Center and Shelter&lt;/a&gt; is a point of interest.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the Litago area?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, there are more challenging options. The region features 13 difficult routes, including summit ascents. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40309588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit loop from San Martín Urbanización&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a significant elevation gain and takes you to the summit of Moncayo.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Litago?
A: The trails around Litago are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.7 out of 5 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the beech forests, and the sense of being immersed in a protected natural environment.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking trails near Litago?
A: Information regarding specific public transport routes directly to trailheads is limited. It is generally recommended to use a car to access the various starting points for hikes within Moncayo Natural Park, as this offers the most flexibility for exploring the region's diverse trails.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Litago?
A: While Litago itself is a small village, within Moncayo Natural Park and nearby areas, you can find options for refreshments and accommodation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Moncayo Restaurant and Hostel&lt;/a&gt; is one such option, offering both food and lodging. There are also several mountain shelters like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6255491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majada Baja Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5310671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrogordo Refuge&lt;/a&gt; for those planning longer excursions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Moncayo Natural Park?
A: Moncayo Natural Park offers hiking opportunities throughout the year, but the best time largely depends on your preferences. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with autumn being particularly stunning due to the changing colors of the beech forests. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter offers a different experience with potential snow, especially at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by a mountain shelter or refuge?
A: Yes, some trails incorporate mountain shelters. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972105181/refugio-valdemanzano-and-barranco-de-la-morca-circular-from-the-electric-central" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Valdemanzano and Barranco de la Morca - circular from the Electric Central&lt;/a&gt; route leads past the Valdemanzano mountain shelter, offering a potential resting point or a destination for your hike.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in total around Litago?
A: There are over 60 hiking routes available around Litago, providing a wide array of choices for exploring Moncayo Natural Park. These routes cater to various difficulty levels and offer diverse scenic experiences.
